```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Density Surge in Sunbelt States",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API for daily EV charger count changes in TX, FL, AZ, NV regions",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid expansion of EV charging infrastructure signals accelerating capital deployment and grid stress in high-growth regions, often preceding utility rate pressure and industrial construction demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Infrastructure buildout drives industrial demand for construction, electrical equipment, and smart grid technology."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly charger additions exceed 200-unit threshold in any tracked Sunbelt metro, enter long",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if weekly additions drop below 100 units",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "EV charger deployment accelerates seasonally in Q1 and Q2; regional clusters frequently hit thresholds as utilities and private firms push infrastructure rollout."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Storage Draw Reversal Signal",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series IMNRNSA (US EIA natural gas storage) weekly readings",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Unexpected reversal from net storage draws to net builds signals demand destruction or supply shock, triggering rapid utility and commodity sector repricing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Reversal in gas storage trends signals weakening energy demand, refinery utilization pressure, and margin compression."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 4-week moving average of storage draws flips from negative to positive, short XLE",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if 4-week MA reverses back negative",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Storage injection/withdrawal cycles flip seasonally and during demand shocks; reversals occur multiple times annually in response to weather and economic cycles."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends Surge in 'Pest Control Failure' Searches",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly interest for 'cockroach infestation', 'bed bug removal', 'rat exterminator' in major metros via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in pest control search interest signal housing stock deterioration, seasonal humidity/warmth events, or urban sanitation breakdown—all leading indicators of local property stress and rental market weakness.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Pest control search surges correlate with deferred maintenance, rental unit degradation, and downward property value pressure."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly search index for any major metro exceeds 150% of 52-week median, short XLRE",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if search index falls back below 110% of median",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal temperature swings and humidity cycles trigger pest searches monthly; urban deterioration and seasonal transitions ensure regular threshold breaches."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Hospital Admission Surge Detection via Google Trends",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly interest for 'emergency room wait time', 'hospital admission process', 'urgent care near me' via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in hospital/ER search interest signal acute health crisis events (seasonal flu, contamination events, heatwaves) that precede healthcare utilization surges and pharmaceutical demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLV",
      "rationale": "Hospital admission spikes drive emergency pharmaceutical demand, clinical supplies, and diagnostic testing—all benefiting healthcare sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly search index exceeds 130% of 52-week median across 3+ major metros, go long XLV",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if search index normalized to <110% of median",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal illness cycles, heatwave events, and contamination incidents trigger hospital search spikes multiple times per year, especially during winter and summer peaks."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Construction Equipment Rental Search Surge",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly interest for 'heavy equipment rental', 'excavator rental', 'crane rental' via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true

    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Equipment rental surge precedes capital spending in construction, cement demand, and industrial equipment manufacturing."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly search index exceeds 125% of 13-week moving average, go long XLI",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if index falls to 105% of moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal construction cycles, permit issuance waves, and weather-driven project starts ensure equipment rental searches spike multiple times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Weather-Driven HVAC Repair Search Spike",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily temperature extremes (>95F or <20F) + Google Trends weekly 'air conditioner repair', 'furnace repair' searches via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily|weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Extreme temperature events combined with HVAC repair search surges signal replacement cycle acceleration, driving demand for electrical equipment, installation labor, and utility infrastructure upgrades.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "HVAC replacement drives electrical grid demand, utility capex, and peak demand pricing pressure."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If temperature extreme occurs + weekly HVAC search index >140% of median within same week, go long XLU",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if search index normalizes and temperatures moderate",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Extreme heat/cold events occur seasonally; winter and summer temperature swings reliably trigger HVAC repair searches multiple times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Small Package Delivery RSS Volume Surge",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count for 'UPS delivery delay', 'FedEx package delay', 'Amazon logistics slowdown' via rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sustained RSS mention spikes for delivery delays signal capacity constraints, labor shortages, or seasonal volume compression in parcel logistics, pressuring shipping and retail margins.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Delivery delays reduce consumer satisfaction, compress retail margins, and signal e-commerce logistics stress."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for delivery delay articles exceeds 15 for 3 consecutive days, short XLY",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if daily RSS count falls below 8 for 5 consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Holiday shopping, seasonal weather events, and labor staffing issues trigger delivery delay articles regularly; RSS spikes occur every few weeks during peak seasons."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Tech Layoff Announcement Velocity",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count for 'technology company layoff announcement', 'tech sector hiring freeze', 'software engineer severance' via rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in tech layoff announcement frequency signal macro weakness, margin compression, and capital allocation collapse—driving sector rotation away from high-growth tech toward value.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Tech layoff waves precede earnings misses, valuation compression, and reduced enterprise IT spending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for layoff announcements exceeds 12 for 2 consecutive days, short XLK",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count remains below 6 for 10 consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Tech sector earnings cycles, macro recessions, and strategic restructuring announcements generate layoff news regularly; thresholds breach multiple times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Restaurant Expansion Announcement Clustering",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count for 'restaurant chain opening', 'new restaurant location announcement', 'restaurant franchise expansion' via rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Clusters of restaurant expansion announcements signal optimistic consumer outlook, real estate availability, and labor market confidence—bullish signals for consumer discretionary and hospitality.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Restaurant expansion waves signal consumer confidence, foot traffic expectations, and real estate market health."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for restaurant expansion announcements exceeds 10 for 3 consecutive days, go long XLY",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count falls below 6 for 5 consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Quarterly earnings announcements and franchise disclosure documents release expansion plans in clusters; seasonal tourism and holiday planning drive announcement waves regularly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Fuel Surcharge Announcement Spike",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count for 'airline fuel surcharge', 'airline ticket price increase fuel', 'carrier surcharge announcement' via rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in airline surcharge announcements signal crude oil pressure and margin compression in carriers, triggering flight capacity cuts and reduced travel demand—bearish for discretionary travel and hospitality.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Fuel surcharge announcements signal rising operational costs, capacity constraint, and reduced pricing power—reducing leisure travel demand."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for surcharge announcements exceeds 8 for 2 consecutive days, short XLY",
      "exit": "After 2 weeks or if daily count falls below 4 for 7 consecutive days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Oil price volatility and quarterly fare review cycles trigger surcharge announcement waves multiple times per year; seasonal travel demand spikes force pricing announcement clusters."

]
```
